home.title: Tortellino d'Oro | Restaurant · Fresh Pasta in Milan
home.description: An author's trattoria in the heart of Milan: pasta hand-rolled every morning, seasonal ingredients, tradition revisited with restraint.

menu.title: The Menu | Tortellino d'Oro
menu.description: Starters, hand-rolled fresh pasta, mains and house desserts. The Tortellino d'Oro menu, between tradition and season.

about.title: About Us | Tortellino d'Oro
about.description: Our origins, our philosophy and the daily ritual of the sfoglia: the story of Tortellino d'Oro, an author's trattoria in Milan.

contact.title: Contact | Tortellino d'Oro
contact.description: Book a table or write to us: address, opening hours, phone number and how to reach Tortellino d'Oro in Milan.

chapters.title: Every morning at six
chapters.body: Fresh eggs, carefully chosen flour, the wooden board still cold. Before the city wakes, the workshop already smells of pasta dough.

chapters.title: The sfoglia
chapters.body: Rolled by hand until it lets the light through. That's where the hand shows, and that's where the whole dish is decided.

chapters.title: The dish
chapters.body: The broth has been simmering for eight hours; the tortellini go in last. The rest is the time you take at the table.

dishes.name: Tortellini in capon broth
dishes.description: Paper-thin pasta, the classic filling, an eight-hour broth.

dishes.name: Tagliatelle with white ragù
dishes.description: A tomato-free courtyard ragù, slowly cooked in white wine.

dishes.name: Pumpkin and amaretti tortelli
dishes.description: Mantuan pumpkin, amaretti, brown butter and sage.

experience.title: A dining room that speaks softly
experience.body: Dark wood, handmade ceramics, raw linen and a light that warms without glaring. Forty seats, not one more: every table has its own space and its own pace.

experience2.title: Every detail has a maker
experience2.body: The ceramics come from a single potter's wheel; the linen is woven a few kilometres away. Nothing in the room is anonymous: behind every object there is a name.

menuPage.title: The Menu
menuPage.subtitle: A short menu, chosen with the seasons. All our pasta is born in our workshop.
menuPage.legendAllergens: Allergens are listed under each dish. For severe intolerances, please tell our staff.
menuPage.pastaNote: All our pasta is fresh, hand-rolled in our own workshop.

items.name: Hand-cut Fassona beef, hazelnuts and Castelmagno
items.description: Knife-cut, Ligurian olive oil, shavings of alpine Castelmagno.

items.name: Charcoal-grilled artichoke, savoury zabaione
items.description: Artichoke heart cooked over the flame, Parmigiano zabaione.

items.name: Culatello di Zibello with gnocco fritto
items.description: Aged 24 months, gnocco fritto fried to order.

items.name: Tortellini in capon broth
items.description: The dish we are named after. Paper-thin pasta, an eight-hour broth.

items.name: Tagliatelle with white ragù
items.description: A tomato-free courtyard ragù, slowly cooked in white wine.

items.name: Pumpkin and amaretti tortelli
items.description: Mantuan pumpkin, amaretti, brown butter and crisp sage.

items.name: Tagliolini with lemon and bottarga
items.description: The thinnest of pasta, lemon butter, grey mullet bottarga.

items.name: Potato gnocchi, red wine and gorgonzola
items.description: Featherlight gnocchi, red wine reduction, sweet gorgonzola DOP.

items.name: Beef cheek braised in Barbera
items.description: Twelve hours of slow cooking, mashed potatoes with alpine butter.

items.name: Guinea fowl stuffed with chestnuts
items.description: Breast and leg, chestnut and sausage stuffing, dark jus.

items.name: Whipped salt cod, charred polenta
items.description: Whipped with Ligurian olive oil, heritage-corn polenta off the grill.

items.name: Charcoal-grilled seasonal vegetables
items.description: From the morning market, olive oil and Cervia sea salt.

items.name: Mashed potatoes with alpine butter
items.description: Whipped to order, as generous as it should be.

items.name: Field greens, aged wine vinegar
items.description: Bitter herbs and crisp leaves, dressed at the table.

items.name: Zuppa inglese, served by the spoonful
Hazelnut cake, Moscato zabaione
items.description: Piedmont hazelnut IGP, flourless, warm zabaione.

items.name: Lemon and olive oil sorbet
items.description: Sorrento lemons, extra-virgin olive oil, Maldon salt.

wine.name: Wine List
wine.body: Around one hundred and fifty labels, with a soft spot for small producers from the Langhe, Oltrepò and the Emilian Apennines. The full list is available in the dining room — ask us, we love talking about it.

sections.title: Where we come from
sections.body: [PLACEHOLDER — the real story: family, opening year, territory. Tone: "It all begins in a small-town kitchen, where on Sundays the pasta dough covered the whole table. In 20XX that tradition moved to Milan, with one promise: do few things, do them every day, do them by hand."]

sections.title: Tradition revisited, with restraint
sections.body: [PLACEHOLDER — suppliers and seasonality. Tone: "Revisiting doesn't mean distorting: it means removing the unnecessary and listening to the season. Our suppliers are names and faces, not price lists: the Parmigiano comes from a single dairy."]

sections.title: The ritual of the rolling pin
sections.body: [PLACEHOLDER — the daily ritual: who rolls it, when, how. Tone: "At seven in the morning the workshop already smells of flour. The dough is rolled until it lets the light through: that's where the whole dish is decided."]
